message:
fix(self-test): enforce per-worker total timeout + classify timed-out summary (bd-nuuqt)

`rch self-test --all` could silently wedge on one slow worker even
when `--timeout 120` was set at the CLI. The timeout was applied to
each individual attempt inside the retry loop, but the loop itself
had no outer budget — so a worker that timed out 3 times in a row
consumed `3 * timeout + 2 * retry_delay` of wall clock before the
record was finalized, which in practice looked like "hung forever"
from the agent's perspective.

Follow-up of the previously-filed bd-w5r9 ("self-test command hangs
indefinitely with no output"); css surfaced 30+ hits on "Worker
self-test failed" without timeout context. Confirmed against a
7-worker fleet — single-worker mode honored `--timeout` cleanly, but
`--all` did not.

### `rchd/src/self_test.rs`

`run_single_worker_test` is now structured as two layered timeouts:

  1. **Per attempt** (unchanged semantics): each call to
     `RemoteCompilationTest::run()` is wrapped in
     `tokio::time::timeout(options.timeout, ...)`.

  2. **Per worker total** (new): the retry loop itself is wrapped
     in `tokio::time::timeout(options.timeout, ...)`. This makes
     `options.timeout` a hard upper bound on total wall clock for
     a single worker, regardless of retry count. A wedged worker
     can no longer starve `--all` of attention.

On the per-worker total timeout, the returned record is stamped
with an error message prefixed `[RCH-E203]` so downstream automation
can branch on a stable code (the catalog already defines
`WorkerSelfTestFailed = RCH-E203`) instead of substring-matching on
"timed out".

The `_pool` parameter is preserved with a `_` prefix for future use
(status updates during the run).

### `rch/src/commands/status.rs` (`rch self-test` CLI renderer)

Added a final one-line summary after per-worker lines:

    Summary: N ok, M timed out, K failed.

Timeout classification is done by inspecting `result.error` for
`[RCH-E203]` or a case-insensitive "timed out" substring so it
catches both the new stable-code path and any legacy formatting.
The three counts use distinct styles (success/warning/error) and
fall back to muted "0" when that bucket is empty, so an agent
watching `--all` output can tell at a glance whether a failure is
a transient wedged worker (timeouts) or a real regression (failed).

The default `--timeout` is deliberately left at 300s so slower
builder workers aren't regressed; this change only tightens what
"timeout" means operationally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -539,8 +539,21 @@ fn parse_duration(value: &str) -> Option<Duration> {
     humantime::parse_duration(value).ok()
 }
 
-/// Run a single worker self-test with retries and timeout enforcement.
-/// This is a standalone function to allow parallel execution via tokio::spawn.
+/// Run a single worker self-test with retries, each bounded by the per-worker
+/// `options.timeout`, and with a fleet-safe total budget so a wedged worker
+/// can never starve `--all` mode of attention (bd-nuuqt).
+///
+/// The `options.timeout` value is enforced in two places:
+///
+/// 1. **Per attempt**: each call to the remote compilation test is wrapped in
+///    `tokio::time::timeout(options.timeout, ...)`.
+/// 2. **Per worker total**: the retry loop itself is wrapped in
+///    `tokio::time::timeout(options.timeout, ...)`. This guarantees the total
+///    wall clock time a single worker can consume is strictly bounded,
+///    regardless of retry count.
+///
+/// On timeout, `RCH-E203` (`WorkerSelfTestFailed`) is prefixed to the error
+/// message so downstream automation can branch on a stable code.
 async fn run_single_worker_test(
     run_id: u64,
     worker: &WorkerConfig,
@@ -549,62 +562,85 @@ async fn run_single_worker_test(
     config: &SelfTestConfig,
     _pool: &WorkerPool, // Reserved for future use (e.g., status updates)
 ) -> SelfTestResultRecord {
-    let retry_delay =
-        parse_duration(&config.retry_delay).unwrap_or_else(|| Duration::from_secs(300));
-
-    let mut attempt = 0;
-    loop {
-        attempt += 1;
-        let test = RemoteCompilationTest::new(worker.clone(), project_path.to_path_buf())
-            .with_timeout(options.timeout)
-            .with_release_mode(options.release_mode);
-
-        // CRITICAL: Actually enforce the timeout using tokio::time::timeout
-        // The RemoteCompilationTest.timeout field wasn't being applied!
-        let result = tokio::time::timeout(options.timeout, test.run()).await;
-
-        let record = match result {
-            Ok(Ok(outcome)) => SelfTestResultRecord {
-                run_id,
-                worker_id: worker.id.to_string(),
-                passed: outcome.success,
-                local_hash: Some(outcome.local_hash.code_hash),
-                remote_hash: Some(outcome.remote_hash.code_hash),
-                local_time_ms: Some(outcome.local_build_ms),
-                remote_time_ms: Some(outcome.compilation_ms),
-                error: outcome.error,
-            },
-            Ok(Err(err)) => SelfTestResultRecord {
-                run_id,
-                worker_id: worker.id.to_string(),
-                passed: false,
-                local_hash: None,
-                remote_hash: None,
-                local_time_ms: None,
-                remote_time_ms: None,
-                error: Some(err.to_string()),
-            },
-            Err(_elapsed) => SelfTestResultRecord {
-                run_id,
-                worker_id: worker.id.to_string(),
-                passed: false,
-                local_hash: None,
-                remote_hash: None,
-                local_time_ms: None,
-                remote_time_ms: None,
-                error: Some(format!("Self-test timed out after {:?}", options.timeout)),
-            },
-        };
+    let worker_id = worker.id.to_string();
+    let timeout_total = options.timeout;
+
+    let retry_loop = async {
+        let retry_delay =
+            parse_duration(&config.retry_delay).unwrap_or_else(|| Duration::from_secs(300));
+
+        let mut attempt = 0u32;
+        loop {
+            attempt += 1;
+            let test = RemoteCompilationTest::new(worker.clone(), project_path.to_path_buf())
+                .with_timeout(options.timeout)
+                .with_release_mode(options.release_mode);
 
-        if record.passed || attempt > config.retry_count {
-            return record;
+            let result = tokio::time::timeout(options.timeout, test.run()).await;
+
+            let record = match result {
+                Ok(Ok(outcome)) => SelfTestResultRecord {
+                    run_id,
+                    worker_id: worker_id.clone(),
+                    passed: outcome.success,
+                    local_hash: Some(outcome.local_hash.code_hash),
+                    remote_hash: Some(outcome.remote_hash.code_hash),
+                    local_time_ms: Some(outcome.local_build_ms),
+                    remote_time_ms: Some(outcome.compilation_ms),
+                    error: outcome.error,
+                },
+                Ok(Err(err)) => SelfTestResultRecord {
+                    run_id,
+                    worker_id: worker_id.clone(),
+                    passed: false,
+                    local_hash: None,
+                    remote_hash: None,
+                    local_time_ms: None,
+                    remote_time_ms: None,
+                    error: Some(err.to_string()),
+                },
+                Err(_elapsed) => SelfTestResultRecord {
+                    run_id,
+                    worker_id: worker_id.clone(),
+                    passed: false,
+                    local_hash: None,
+                    remote_hash: None,
+                    local_time_ms: None,
+                    remote_time_ms: None,
+                    error: Some(format!(
+                        "[RCH-E203] Self-test attempt timed out after {:?}",
+                        options.timeout
+                    )),
+                },
+            };
+
+            if record.passed || attempt > config.retry_count {
+                return record;
+            }
+
+            warn!(
+                "Self-test failed for worker {} (attempt {}/{}), retrying in {:?}",
+                worker_id, attempt, config.retry_count, retry_delay
+            );
+            sleep(retry_delay).await;
         }
+    };
 
-        warn!(
-            "Self-test failed for worker {} (attempt {}/{}), retrying in {:?}",
-            worker.id, attempt, config.retry_count, retry_delay
-        );
-        sleep(retry_delay).await;
+    match tokio::time::timeout(timeout_total, retry_loop).await {
+        Ok(record) => record,
+        Err(_elapsed) => SelfTestResultRecord {
+            run_id,
+            worker_id,
+            passed: false,
+            local_hash: None,
+            remote_hash: None,
+            local_time_ms: None,
+            remote_time_ms: None,
+            error: Some(format!(
+                "[RCH-E203] Self-test hit per-worker deadline {:?} (including retries)",
+                timeout_total
+            )),
+        },
     }
 }
